illinois.h2: Where Illinois talcum powder cases are handled
illinois.body: Illinois residents' federal talcum powder claims are coordinated in MDL-2738 before Judge Michael A. Shipp in the U.S. District Court for the District of New Jersey — the largest active MDL by pending actions (about 68,029 as of June 1, 2026). After Johnson & Johnson's three 'Texas Two-Step' bankruptcy attempts were rejected, most recently on March 31, 2025, tens of thousands of cases returned to active dockets. For Illinois claimants, the practical focus is documentation: the talc product used, years of use, and pathology confirming ovarian cancer or mesothelioma.

sources.label: JPML — Pending MDL Dockets by Actions Pending, June 1, 2026 (MDL-2738, Judge Shipp, 68,029)
Drugwatch — third J&J talc bankruptcy (Red River) denied March 31, 2025

faqs.question: Where are Illinois talcum powder cases handled?
faqs.answer: Illinois residents' federal talc cases are coordinated in MDL-2738 before Judge Michael A. Shipp in the U.S. District Court for the District of New Jersey. Ovarian-cancer and mesothelioma cases may also be tried in state court. No global settlement exists, so individual eligibility is fact-specific.

missouri.body: Missouri produced the largest talcum powder verdict on record. In Ingham v. Johnson & Johnson, a St. Louis jury awarded about $4.69 billion in 2018 to 22 women with ovarian cancer; on appeal the Missouri Court of Appeals reduced the judgment to roughly $2.1 billion and trimmed the plaintiff group on jurisdictional grounds, and in 2021 the U.S. Supreme Court declined to hear J&J's appeal — leaving the reduced award as a final result. Missouri residents' federal talc cases are otherwise coordinated in MDL-2738 in the District of New Jersey.

sources.label: Missouri Court of Appeals — Ingham v. Johnson & Johnson opinion (June 23, 2020)
U.S. Supreme Court — Ingham docket No. 20-1223 (cert. denied June 1, 2021)

faqs.question: What was the Ingham talcum powder verdict in Missouri?
faqs.answer: In Ingham v. Johnson & Johnson, a St. Louis jury awarded about $4.69 billion in 2018 to 22 women with ovarian cancer. The Missouri Court of Appeals reduced it to roughly $2.1 billion in 2020, and the U.S. Supreme Court declined review in 2021, making the reduced award final. Past verdicts do not predict any individual outcome.

texas.h2: Texas and the Red River talc bankruptcy
texas.body: Texas has a distinctive place in the talc litigation: it is where Johnson & Johnson made its third and final 'Texas Two-Step' bankruptcy attempt. J&J's subsidiary Red River Talc LLC filed a prepackaged Chapter 11 — built around a proposed roughly $9 billion plan — in the U.S. Bankruptcy Court for the Southern District of Texas, and Judge Christopher Lopez denied confirmation on March 31, 2025. With that route closed, Texas residents' federal talc cases proceed in MDL-2738 in the District of New Jersey, and individual cases are again being tried.

sources.label: Drugwatch — Red River Talc Chapter 11 denied (Houston, March 31, 2025)

faqs.question: What is Texas's role in the talcum powder litigation?
faqs.answer: Texas is where Johnson & Johnson's third bankruptcy attempt played out: Red River Talc LLC's prepackaged Chapter 11, built around a proposed roughly $9 billion plan, was denied by a Houston bankruptcy judge on March 31, 2025. Texas residents' federal talc claims are coordinated in MDL-2738 in New Jersey.

florida.body: Florida has seen cosmetic-talc cases reach trial with mixed results for plaintiffs. In a 2024 Sarasota County case, Matthey v. Johnson & Johnson, the jury returned a defense verdict, finding J&J's talc was not a legal cause of the plaintiff's ovarian cancer — reported as one of the first cosmetic-talc cases before a Florida jury. Florida residents' federal talc cases are coordinated in MDL-2738 in the District of New Jersey, where the bulk of the ovarian-cancer docket is centralized.

sources.label: Courtroom View Network — Florida jury clears J&J in cosmetic-talc trial (2024)

faqs.question: What happened in Florida's talcum powder trial?
faqs.answer: In a 2024 Sarasota County case (Matthey v. Johnson & Johnson), the jury returned a defense verdict for J&J — one of the first cosmetic-talc cases before a Florida jury. Trial outcomes vary by case and jurisdiction, and a defense verdict in one case does not determine any other claim.

california.body: California has produced significant mesothelioma verdicts tied to talc. In Leavitt v. Johnson & Johnson, an Alameda County jury awarded about $29.4 million to a woman with mesothelioma, and a California Court of Appeal affirmed the award in 2021; another Alameda County case, Prudencio, produced a verdict of roughly $26.6 million. California residents' federal talc cases are coordinated in MDL-2738 in the District of New Jersey, while mesothelioma cases like these are tried in state court.

sources.label: NPR — Johnson & Johnson hit with $29M mesothelioma verdict (Leavitt)
Kazan Law — $26.5M mesothelioma verdict for a J&J Baby Powder user (Prudencio)

faqs.question: Have California juries decided talcum powder cases?
faqs.answer: Yes. California juries have returned mesothelioma verdicts against Johnson & Johnson — about $29.4 million in Leavitt v. Johnson & Johnson (affirmed on appeal in 2021) and roughly $26.6 million in the Prudencio case. Results vary by case, and past verdicts do not predict any individual outcome.

north-carolina.h2: Where North Carolina talcum powder cases are handled
north-carolina.body: North Carolina residents' federal talcum powder claims are coordinated in MDL-2738 before Judge Michael A. Shipp in the U.S. District Court for the District of New Jersey, the largest active MDL by pending actions (about 68,029 as of June 1, 2026). With Johnson & Johnson's three bankruptcy attempts now rejected — most recently in March 2025 — talc cases have returned to the trial system, and the practical focus for North Carolina claimants is documentation: the product used, years of use, and pathology confirming ovarian cancer or mesothelioma.

faqs.question: Where are North Carolina talcum powder cases handled?
faqs.answer: North Carolina residents' federal talc cases are coordinated in MDL-2738 before Judge Michael A. Shipp in the District of New Jersey. Many ovarian-cancer and mesothelioma cases are also tried in state courts. No global settlement exists, so individual eligibility is fact-specific.

georgia.body: Georgia's first cosmetic-talc case to reach a verdict ended in J&J's favor. In Monroe v. Johnson & Johnson (October 2021), a Richmond County jury cleared Johnson & Johnson in a case alleging that a retired teacher's fatal ovarian cancer was caused by talc. Georgia residents' federal talc cases are coordinated in MDL-2738 in the District of New Jersey, where the bulk of the ovarian-cancer docket is centralized.

sources.label: Courtroom View Network — J&J prevails at trial over Georgia teacher's cancer death (Oct. 2021)

faqs.question: Did a Georgia jury decide a talcum powder case?
faqs.answer: Yes. In Monroe v. Johnson & Johnson (October 2021), a Richmond County jury returned a defense verdict for J&J in a cosmetic-talc case. A defense verdict in one case does not determine any other claim, which turns on its own facts and evidence.

ohio.h2: Where Ohio talcum powder cases are handled
ohio.body: Ohio residents' federal talcum powder claims are coordinated in MDL-2738 before Judge Michael A. Shipp in the U.S. District Court for the District of New Jersey, the country's largest active MDL by pending actions (about 68,029 as of June 1, 2026). With J&J's three bankruptcy attempts rejected — most recently in March 2025 — cases are again proceeding in the trial system. For Ohio claimants, documentation drives review: the talc product, years of use, and pathology confirming ovarian cancer or mesothelioma.

faqs.question: Where are Ohio talcum powder cases handled?
faqs.answer: Ohio residents' federal talc cases are coordinated in MDL-2738 before Judge Michael A. Shipp in the District of New Jersey, with many cases also tried in state court. No global settlement exists, so individual eligibility is fact-specific.

pennsylvania.body: Pennsylvania is an active talc venue. In February 2026, a Philadelphia jury found Johnson & Johnson liable in Emerson v. Johnson & Johnson and awarded $250,000; J&J said it would appeal, so the verdict is not final. Philadelphia's Court of Common Pleas handles complex mass-tort cases, and additional talc cases have been filed there. Pennsylvania residents' federal talc cases are coordinated in MDL-2738 in the District of New Jersey.

faqs.question: Did a Pennsylvania jury rule on a talcum powder case?
faqs.answer: Yes. In February 2026 a Philadelphia jury found Johnson & Johnson liable in Emerson v. Johnson & Johnson and awarded $250,000; J&J said it would appeal, so the verdict is not final. Past verdicts do not predict any individual outcome.
